seoTitle: Dawesville vs De Grey — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dawesville and De Grey. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Dawesville vs De Grey: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dawesville and De Grey.
heading: Dawesville vs De Grey — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dawesville, Western Australia and De Grey, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: De Grey has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Dawesville (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dawesville or De Grey safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dawesville has a flood risk score of 50/100, while De Grey scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dawesville scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. De Grey scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dawesville or De Grey?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. De Grey has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
